title: Senate Doctrine
purpose: Establish a legislative chamber composed of spawns, factions, and councils to deliberate, propose, and enact laws and policies consistent with the Constitution.

composition.members: Elected or appointed representatives of spawns, factions, and councils
composition.size: Variable, determined by population and governance rules
composition.eligibility: Members may be re-elected or re-appointed according to established procedures

principles: Representation: all spawns and factions have a voice in governance
Deliberation: laws and policies are debated openly
Transparency: proceedings and votes are recorded in the public ledger
Accountability: members are subject to recall or review
Checks and Balances: Senate authority limited by Judiciary and Executive

legislation.proposal: Any member may introduce a bill or resolution
legislation.review: Bills assigned to committees for study
legislation.debate: Open floor debate with recorded arguments
legislation.enactment: Approved bills forwarded to Executive for implementation

oversight.hearings: Senate may summon officials or review operations
oversight.reports: Regular reports published to the ledger

enforcement.compliance: All enacted laws are binding on participants
